purpose: Introduce a small but solid generic domain/plugin foundation so integrations such as Blender, FreeCAD, KiCad, Odoo or future domain clients can share the same backend mechanisms for domains, capabilities, policies, schemas, artifacts, RAG profiles, bridge routing, audit and release gates. This track exists to prevent large integrations from becoming hard-coded backend special cases.
goal: Create a generic integration foundation that lets Ananta load and validate domain definitions, capability packs, policy packs, context schemas, artifact types, RAG source profiles and bridge adapter contracts while keeping orchestration, approval, policy and audit centralized in the Hub.
non_goals: Do not build a marketplace or dynamic third-party plugin ecosystem in this track.
Do not allow arbitrary unreviewed Python modules to be loaded as backend plugins.
Do not duplicate client orchestration or policy logic in domain modules.
Do not implement Blender, FreeCAD or KiCad runtime behavior here; only generic mechanisms and minimal example wiring.
Do not introduce Enterprise/KRITIS-specific RBAC or tenancy complexity in this OSS foundation track.
success_criteria: A domain registry can load and validate domain descriptors from configuration files.
Capabilities are declared as data and evaluated generically by Hub services.
Context schemas and artifact types can be registered per domain and validated generically.
Policy packs can be loaded per domain without hard-coding domain rules in service code.
RAG source profiles can be registered per domain and routed to codecompass/rag_helper ingestion and retrieval.
Bridge adapter contracts are defined generically for health, capabilities, context capture, action execution and result reporting.
Audit and approval metadata support domain/capability/action IDs generically.
Release gates can detect false runtime claims for domains without domain-specific hard-coding.
Blender can later consume this foundation instead of creating many blender_* backend special cases.
architecture_guardrails: The Hub remains the authority for orchestration, policy, approval, audit and retrieval.
Domain modules are declarative first: descriptors, schemas, capabilities, policies and RAG profiles before code.
Runtime code extension points must be explicit, reviewed and allow-listed.
No generic mechanism may permit policy or approval bypass.
No domain registry may silently load untrusted executable code.
Generic services must expose degraded and validation-failed states clearly.
Domain descriptors must be versioned and migration-friendly.
Domain-specific clients remain thin and speak stable Ananta APIs.

milestones.title: Registries for domains, capabilities, schemas and artifacts
Generic policy, approval and audit integration
RAG source profile and retrieval integration
Bridge adapter interface and runtime routing

tasks.title: Generic domain integration scope define
acceptance_criteria: Document explains why Ananta needs a generic domain foundation before large integrations become hard-coded.
Document separates generic platform mechanisms from domain-specific descriptors and bridge implementations.
Document explicitly states that this is not a plugin marketplace and not an arbitrary code-loading system.
Document explains how Blender, FreeCAD and KiCad can later share the same foundation.

tasks.title: Domain descriptor schema define
acceptance_criteria: Schema defines domain_id, display_name, version, lifecycle_status, runtime_status, owner, description, supported_clients and source paths.
Schema references capability pack, context schemas, artifact schemas, policy packs, RAG profiles and bridge adapter type.
Schema supports stable versioning and future non-breaking extension fields.
Invalid descriptors fail validation with readable errors.

tasks.title: Domain lifecycle and runtime status model define
acceptance_criteria: Lifecycle states cover planned, foundation_only, runtime_mvp, runtime_complete, deferred, blocked and deprecated.
Runtime status clearly separates descriptor existence from executable behavior.
Status model can be used by release gates to block false runtime claims.
Status model is compatible with existing client surface runtime inventory concepts.

tasks.title: Example minimal domain descriptor add
acceptance_criteria: Example descriptor is intentionally non-runtime or foundation_only.
Example demonstrates descriptor structure without implying a real integration is complete.
Example is used by validation tests.

tasks.title: Domain registry service implement
acceptance_criteria: Service loads domain descriptors from configured domain directories.
Service validates descriptors against schema before exposing them.
Service refuses duplicate domain_id values and malformed descriptors.
Service exposes read-only domain metadata for clients and release gates.
Tests cover valid, invalid, duplicate and missing referenced files.

tasks.title: Capability registry service implement
acceptance_criteria: Capability pack schema defines capability_id, domain_id, display_name, category, risk, read_only, mutating, approval_required and default_policy_state.
Capability IDs use stable names such as blender.scene.read or kicad.drc.run without hard-coded enum lists.
Registry validates capability packs and exposes capabilities by domain and category.
Registry refuses unknown risk levels, duplicate capability IDs and missing domain references.

tasks.title: Context schema registry implement
acceptance_criteria: Registry loads context schemas referenced by domain descriptors.
Registry validates incoming domain context payloads generically.
Validation returns structured accepted, rejected or degraded states.
Tests cover valid payload, oversized payload, malformed payload and unknown domain.

tasks.title: Artifact type registry implement
acceptance_criteria: Artifact type pack schema defines artifact_type_id, domain_id, display_name, schema_ref, render_hint, safety_classification and allowed_clients.
Registry allows TUI/Web/CLI/clients to discover artifact types without hard-coded domain assumptions.
Registry validates artifact payloads against referenced schemas where practical.
Tests cover known, unknown, malformed and unsupported artifact types.

tasks.title: Domain policy pack loader implement
acceptance_criteria: Policy pack schema maps capabilities/actions to allow, deny, approval_required or default_deny decisions.
Loader validates that policy rules reference existing domain capabilities.
Loader supports safe defaults when a policy pack is missing or incomplete.
Tests verify missing policy defaults do not permit unsafe actions.

tasks.title: Generic domain policy evaluation integrate
acceptance_criteria: Policy evaluation accepts domain_id, capability_id, action_id, context summary and actor metadata.
Evaluation returns allow, deny, approval_required or degraded with reasons.
Evaluation is used before execution for any domain action.
Tests cover allowed read-only action, approval-required mutation, denied script execution and unknown capability.

tasks.title: Generic approval metadata extend
acceptance_criteria: Approval requests can include domain_id, capability_id, action_id, expected_effects, risk, artifact_refs and context_hash.
Approval responses can be correlated to domain actions without domain-specific service code.
Stale, malformed or unauthorized approvals remain denied.
Tests verify approval metadata is present for approval-required domain actions.

tasks.title: Generic domain audit event model add
acceptance_criteria: Audit events record domain_id, capability_id, action_id, task_id, approval_id, context_hash, artifact_ids, policy_decision and result status.
Audit distinguishes plan_created, policy_denied, approval_requested, approval_decided, execution_started, execution_finished and execution_failed.
Audit avoids storing full sensitive context unless explicitly allowed by policy.
Tests verify denied and approved flows produce appropriate audit events.

tasks.title: RAG source profile schema define
acceptance_criteria: Schema defines source_id, domain_id, source_type, repo/url/path/ref, allowed_paths, include/exclude globs, license_note, intended_usage and indexing_config_ref.
Schema supports source categories such as api_docs, source_code, examples, project_reference and internal_docs.
Schema requires provenance fields sufficient for audit and explanation.
Schema explicitly supports codecompass/rag_helper as ingestion path.

tasks.title: RAG source profile loader implement
acceptance_criteria: Loader discovers RAG source profiles referenced by domain descriptors.
Loader validates profiles and rejects missing domain references, malformed globs and missing intended usage.
Loader exposes profiles to indexing and retrieval services without requiring domain-specific code.
Tests cover valid, invalid, unknown-domain and missing-index-config cases.

tasks.title: Generic domain retrieval interface define
acceptance_criteria: Retrieval interface accepts domain_id, retrieval_intent, query, context_summary and max_results.
Retrieval returns bounded chunks with source_id, ref/version, path, symbol/section, score/reason and usage limits.
Retrieval refuses unbounded full-repo or full-doc requests by default.
Interface can delegate to existing rag_helper index service or future retrieval backends.

tasks.title: Bridge adapter contract define
acceptance_criteria: Contract defines health, capabilities, capture_context, execute_action, report_result and cancel where practical.
Contract requires correlation IDs, domain_id, capability_id, approval token/reference and result artifact metadata.
Contract states that adapters cannot approve their own actions or bypass Hub policy.
Contract supports local client bridges such as Blender addon bridge without making them backend plugins.

tasks.title: Bridge adapter registry implement
acceptance_criteria: Registry maps domain descriptors to bridge adapter types and allowed communication modes.
Registry does not dynamically import arbitrary executable code from domain directories.
Registry can report missing or unavailable bridges as degraded state.
Tests cover known adapter, unknown adapter, disabled adapter and malformed descriptor.

tasks.title: Generic action routing skeleton add
acceptance_criteria: Router accepts domain action requests and performs validation, policy evaluation and approval-state checks before any execution path.
Router returns plan, denied, approval_required, execution_started or degraded states.
Router refuses unknown domains, unknown capabilities and missing approval for approval-required actions.
Tests verify no route bypasses policy evaluation.

tasks.title: Domain runtime inventory integrate
acceptance_criteria: Inventory records domain status, descriptor paths, required runtime files, smoke commands and evidence references.
Inventory can mark domains as planned, foundation_only, runtime_mvp, runtime_complete, deferred or blocked.
Inventory does not allow descriptor-only domains to be treated as runtime-complete.
Tests verify status counters and referenced domain IDs remain consistent.

tasks.title: Generic domain audit script add
acceptance_criteria: Audit script verifies domain descriptors, capability packs, schemas, policies, RAG profiles and inventory entries are internally consistent.
Audit fails on missing required files for runtime_mvp/runtime_complete domains.
Audit fails on skeleton-only or docs-only domains claiming runtime_mvp.
Audit is usable by release gates.

tasks.title: Release gate integration for domains add
acceptance_criteria: Release gate runs generic domain integration audit when domain inventory exists.
Gate separates blocking automated checks from manual/advisory smoke evidence.
Gate blocks false runtime claims for domains without per-domain hard-coding.
Gate output lists blockers and deferred domains clearly.

tasks.title: Blender descriptor alignment plan add
acceptance_criteria: Document maps todo.blender.json tasks to generic domain foundation mechanisms where possible.
Document identifies which planned blender_* backend services should become generic services plus Blender descriptors.
Document keeps Blender-specific context, bridge and policy details clearly separated.
Document prevents the Blender track from duplicating generic registry/policy/retrieval logic.

tasks.title: Example Blender foundation descriptor add
acceptance_criteria: Descriptor marks Blender honestly as planned or foundation_only until runtime evidence exists.
Capabilities include at least scene.read, selection.read, preview.render, script.plan and script.execute with correct safety flags.
Descriptor references future schemas/policies/RAG profiles without claiming runtime completion.
Generic audit accepts the descriptor only at its honest lifecycle status.

tasks.title: Generic domain foundation docs write
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain how to add a new domain descriptor, capability pack, schema, policy pack and RAG profile.
Docs include minimal examples and warning notes about not loading arbitrary code.
Docs explain how release gates determine whether a domain is runtime-ready.
Docs mention Blender as first large consumer but not as the only target.

tasks.title: Post-foundation cleanup todo generate
acceptance_criteria: Cleanup todo lists remaining generic gaps, Blender alignment gaps and deferred FreeCAD/KiCad preparation gaps.
Cleanup distinguishes missing implementation, weak tests, documentation drift and future enhancements.
Cleanup does not pull Enterprise/KRITIS scope into the OSS foundation.

execution_order_summary: Stage 1: Define generic domain scope and descriptor contracts.
Stage 2: Implement registries for domains, capabilities, context schemas and artifact types.
Stage 3: Integrate generic policy, approval and audit metadata for domain actions.
Stage 4: Add RAG source profile loading and generic retrieval interface for codecompass/rag_helper-backed domain knowledge.
Stage 5: Define bridge adapter contract and generic action routing skeleton.
Stage 6: Add domain runtime inventory, audit script and release-gate checks.
Stage 7: Align Blender with the generic foundation and document the model.

GEN-M2.title: Registries for domains, capabilities, schemas and artifacts

GEN-M3.title: Generic policy, approval and audit integration

GEN-M4.title: RAG source profile and retrieval integration

GEN-M5.title: Bridge adapter interface and runtime routing

notes: tasks_status_summary.by_risk wurde auf die realen Task-Risiken korrigiert (medium=4, high=10, critical=11).
execution_stage_summary ergänzt, damit Rollups je Meilenstein transparent und validator-kompatibel sind.
updated_at/checked_at auf den aktuellen Analysezeitpunkt gesetzt.
Bundle GEN-T01..GEN-T04 umgesetzt und in Tasks/Milestones/Stage-Rollups als done synchronisiert.
Bundles GEN-T05..GEN-T12 umgesetzt und als abgeschlossene Meilensteine GEN-M2 und GEN-M3 synchronisiert.
Bundles GEN-T13..GEN-T18 umgesetzt und als abgeschlossene Meilensteine GEN-M4 und GEN-M5 synchronisiert.
Bundles GEN-T19..GEN-T25 umgesetzt und als abgeschlossene Meilensteine GEN-M6 und GEN-M7 synchronisiert.

summary_notes: This track should be implemented before or alongside the Blender integration to avoid hard-coded blender_* backend services where generic domain mechanisms are sufficient.
The foundation is intentionally smaller than a marketplace: descriptors and allow-listed adapter contracts first, dynamic third-party plugin loading later if ever.
The generic model should strengthen Ananta's core positioning as a controlled AI control plane rather than a collection of one-off tool integrations.
FreeCAD and KiCad can later reuse this foundation with stricter domain-specific policies and correctness gates.
Summary counters and milestone stage rollups are now synchronized and explicitly documented.
Bundle 1 now provides scope docs, descriptor/status schemas, and a descriptor-only example domain baseline.
Bundles 2 and 3 now provide generic registries plus policy/approval/audit contracts without introducing domain-specific orchestration logic.
Bundles 4 and 5 now add generic RAG profile loading/retrieval plus bridge contract/registry/routing skeleton under hub-governed policy checks.
Bundles 6 and 7 now complete runtime inventory + domain audit + release-gate integration and add Blender alignment/docs/cleanup follow-up planning.

end_summaries.title: Analyseergebnis
items: Die Datei ist strukturell als Task-Track konsistent aufgebaut und deckt Scope, Milestones, Critical Path und Abhängigkeiten klar ab.
Ein inhaltlicher Drift wurde in tasks_status_summary.by_risk gefunden.

end_summaries.title: Korrekturen
items: by_risk korrigiert auf low=0, medium=4, high=10, critical=11.
updated_at in tasks_status_summary und checked_at in analysis_checks aktualisiert.

end_summaries.title: Verbesserungen und Erweiterungen
items: analysis_checks ergänzt, damit Konsistenzprüfung und Korrekturhistorie nachvollziehbar sind.

end_summaries.title: Status am Ende
items: Alle Umsetzungsbündel abgeschlossen: 0 todo, 0 in_progress, 0 blocked, 25 done (100.0%).
Meilensteine GEN-M1 bis GEN-M7 sind done; der Track ist vollständig umgesetzt und auf Cleanup-Folgearbeiten überführt.
